# MacroLens

> Macro-trading terminal: regime analysis, Fed policy, yield curves, inflation,
> cross-asset confirmation, Forecast Lab (HMM + XGBoost) diagnostics, economic
> calendar, and LLM-backed market intelligence. FastAPI backend + Next.js frontend.

The HTTP API is served by the FastAPI backend under the `/api` prefix. Interactive,
always-current docs: `/docs` (Swagger UI) and `/openapi.json` (machine-readable schema).
Health: `GET /api/health`.

## API surface

- [Indicators](/api/indicators): macro indicators, z-scores, trends
- [Fed Policy](/api/fed): rate path, FedWatch, SEP, dashboard
- [Yield Curve](/api/yield-curve): curve snapshots, spreads, inversions
- [Navigator](/api/navigator): regime navigator, phase confidence, cross-asset confirmation
- [Regime](/api/regime): macro regime / quadrant state and history
- [Forecast Lab](/api/forecast-lab): HMM + GBDT forecasts, ensembles, training, snapshots
- [ML Regime](/api/ml): ML regime prediction, backtest, metrics, training
- [Market](/api/market): market data, prices, breadth, relative performance
- [Calendar](/api/calendar): economic calendar, events, briefings, FOMC
- [Calendar Canary](/api/calendar-canary): forward calendar data-quality canary
- [Alerts](/api/alerts): user alerts and notifications
- [Agents](/api/agents): LLM intelligence agents (Fed / News / Macro / Master)
- [Memory](/api/memory): embedding-based memory retrieval

## Deployment

- [DEPLOY.md](/DEPLOY.md): Vercel (frontend) + Hugging Face Spaces (backend) + managed Postgres/Redis

## Notes

- The backend is always-on (APScheduler refresh jobs + ML training); it is not serverless.
- LLM agents and embeddings are optional; without API keys the system uses rule-based fallbacks.
